```javascript
function solve(nums) {
    const N = nums.length;
    const MAX_VAL = 100000;

    // 1. Find U, the set of unique elements in nums.
    const uniqueNumsSet = new Set(nums);
    let uniqueNums = Array.from(uniqueNumsSet);

    // 2. Sort U to ensure we find the smallest divisor first.
    uniqueNums.sort((a, b) => a - b);

    // 3. Initialize min_d array. We use MAX_VAL + 1 as sentinel.
    const min_d = new Array(MAX_VAL + 1).fill(MAX_VAL + 1);

    // 4. Precalculate the smallest divisor in U for every number up to MAX_VAL.
    for (const u of uniqueNums) {
        // u is the potential smallest divisor.
        // Iterate through all multiples of u up to MAX_VAL.
        for (let i = u; i <= MAX_VAL; i += u) {
            // Since we iterate u in increasing order, the first time min_d[i] is set
            // or if min_d[i] is currently greater than u, we update it.
            // Because we initialized to MAX_VAL + 1, and u <= MAX_VAL, this condition handles both unset and update cases correctly.
            if (min_d[i] > u) {
                min_d[i] = u;
            }
        }
    }

    // 5. Calculate the final sum.
    let totalSum = 0;
    for (const x of nums) {
        // x is guaranteed to be <= MAX_VAL
        totalSum += min_d[x];
    }

    return totalSum;
}
```
